us-politics.headline: F-15 Downed Over Iran, One Crew Member Still Missing
us-politics.body:
Iran shot down a US F-15 fighter jet during ongoing military operations, with one crew member rescued and a search underway for the second. The incident marks a sharp escalation in the US-Iran conflict and has drawn international attention, including Austria banning US military aircraft from its airspace in protest.

International law experts have written to Congress arguing the US strikes on Iran may constitute war crimes, while Senator Van Hollen pushed back on administration justifications, saying Iran has no ICBM capable of reaching the US. Supporters of the strikes argue Iran posed a credible regional threat and that decisive action was necessary.

Podcasters Joe Rogan and Theo Von, both with large audiences on the right, publicly criticised Trump's decision to attack Iran - a sign that opposition to the war is not strictly partisan.

sources.title: Live updates: US rescues one F-15 crew member after Iran downs jet, searches for second
1 rescued after U.S. fighter jet crash in Iran; search ongoing for crew member
Austria bans US military planes from its airspace
International law experts: US strikes on Iran may have amounted to war crimes
US pilot rescued from downed F-15 fighter jet in Iran, search for second crew member ongoing
Van Hollen: Iran ‘nowhere near’ any ICBM that can reach US
Rogan, Theo Von slam Trump’s move to attack Iran

us-politics.headline: Trump's $1.5 Trillion Defense Budget Cuts Domestic Programs
us-politics.body:
The White House has submitted a 2027 budget requesting a record $1.5 trillion for the Pentagon, a figure that would represent the largest defense appropriation in US history. The proposal pairs that increase with cuts to domestic programs, including another attempt to eliminate LIHEAP, the low-income home heating assistance program.

House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries accused Trump of hollowing out household finances through his broader economic agenda. Republicans have framed the defense buildup as essential to national security amid active conflict in the Middle East and rising global threats.

Trump also proposed a separate $10 billion fund for Washington DC construction and beautification projects, a priority that critics argue sits awkwardly alongside cuts to programs serving low-income Americans.

sources.title: Trump requests record-breaking budget of $1.5 trillion for Pentagon
Trump seeks $1.5 trillion for defense spending in 2027 budget request
White House requests $1.5 trillion for defense spending in 2027 budget
Trump re-ups push to eliminate low-income heating assistance program LIHEAP
Trump proposes $10B fund for D.C. construction, beautification projects
Jeffries: ‘What Trump has done is decimate the bank accounts of the American people’

us-politics.headline: Cabinet Turbulence: Bondi Fired, Army Chief Ousted, More Pressure Ahead
us-politics.body:
Attorney General Pam Bondi was fired despite her consistent alignment with Trump's agenda, signalling that loyalty alone is no longer sufficient protection in the current White House. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth separately fired the Army Chief of Staff, a move that prompted Republican Representative Rich McCormick to say he would investigate immediately.

Kristi Noem has also exited the cabinet, and Fox News reports that further departures are possible as Trump reshapes his team. Trump is now eyeing a new attorney general, though at least one key Republican senator has signalled potential resistance to confirmation.

Anthony Scaramucci, a former Trump aide, argued Bondi was removed because she was not aggressive enough in pursuing Trump's priorities at the Justice Department. The firings raise broader questions about institutional stability heading into the 2026 midterm cycle.

sources.title: Pam Bondi fired despite commitment to Trump agenda: Loyalty not enough!
Trump cabinet shakeup expands after Noem exit, Bondi firing — who’s under pressure next?
GOP rep on Hegseth firing Army chief of staff: ‘I will look into it immediately’
Trump eyes next attorney general as key GOP senator signals potential roadblock
Scaramucci: Bondi wasn’t doing enough for Trump
Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to become acting chief of staff of the US Army

us-politics.headline: Trump Voting Order and DOJ Shake-Up Fuel Democratic Alarm
us-politics.body:
Democrats are raising concerns about two separate fronts. Trump's executive order targeting mail-in voting and establishing a national voter list has drawn criticism from media figures including Katie Couric, who argued the measures are designed to intimidate election workers rather than improve election integrity. Republicans counter that voter roll accuracy is a legitimate administrative goal.

On the legal front, Representative Eric Swalwell denied that Bondi had tipped him off about any internal DOJ report before her firing, pushing back on speculation about the circumstances of her dismissal. A separate opinion piece flagged concerns about the Jack Smith Arctic Frost investigation and what it may reveal about conduct inside the administration.

Taken together, the developments point to a Justice Department in transition at a moment when its independence is under sustained scrutiny from both parties.

sources.title: Couric: Trump mail-in voting order, national voting list part of effort to ‘scare election workers’
Swalwell says Bondi didn’t tip him off on report
What every American needs to know about Jack Smith’s Arctic Frost investigation
Pam Bondi fired despite commitment to Trump agenda: Loyalty not enough!

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Forces Bowen to Defend Australia's Energy Security
au-politics.body:
Australia's fuel reserves are critically low, with industry warning current supplies won't last a month. Energy Minister Chris Bowen has moved to reassure the public, but the gap between government messaging and industry concern is hard to ignore. The crisis has exposed long-standing vulnerabilities in Australia's fuel storage and import capacity.

The shortage is feeding into a broader debate about the Albanese government's energy transition strategy. Critics on the right argue net-zero commitments have left Australia exposed, pointing to continued global oil demand as evidence that renewables cannot yet fill the gap. Supporters counter that the crisis strengthens, not weakens, the case for domestic clean energy investment.

sources.title: Energy minister Chris Bowen on the impact of the fuel crisis - podcast
Net Zero nations trapped in mad race for oil
Fuel heading to Australia won't last a month, industry says

au-politics.headline: Trump's Iran War Reshapes Australian Political Calculations
au-politics.body:
US military action against Iran - including a downed fighter jet with crew rescued - is forcing a reckoning in Canberra. The Albanese government faces pressure to articulate a clearer foreign policy stance as the conflict escalates and Australia's alliance commitments come under scrutiny.

Commentators on the left argue the crisis should spur Albanese toward bolder domestic reforms, reframing Australia's strategic priorities. The right focuses on Trump's assertiveness and the broader question of whether Western nations have the resolve to back their alliances. Both sides agree the conflict marks a turning point. How Albanese responds will shape the election debate.

sources.title: Live: US crew member reportedly rescued after fighter jet downed over Iran
Trump’s Iran war has woken Albanese up to a new reality. Will it spur him towards ‘ambitious’ reforms?
Donald Trump is going on a firing spree

au-politics.headline: Tax Policy Divides Parties as Election Pressure Mounts
au-politics.body:
With an election approaching, both Labor and the Coalition are struggling to land a coherent tax message. The Guardian reports even some Liberals, including Andrew Hastie, are open to higher taxes on miners - a notable shift that signals the political ground is moving. Albanese and Opposition leader Angus Taylor are both chasing voter expectations on cost-of-living relief.

Telstra's price rises are adding to household pressure, with regulators and consumer advocates questioning whether the increases are justified. The political risk for both major parties is being seen as soft on big business while voters are squeezed.

sources.title: Taxing times for Albanese and Taylor as parties seek to match voter expectations
Wake-up call: how Telstra’s ‘unreasonable’ price rises may cause customers to hang up

au-politics.headline: Queensland Man Jailed; Myanmar Junta Chief Seizes Presidency
au-politics.body:
A Queensland man received a 15-year sentence after live-streaming child sexual abuse, with the court making clear the severity of online exploitation offences. The case drew attention to the ongoing challenge of detecting and prosecuting abuse facilitated through digital platforms.

Abroad, Myanmar's junta chief Min Aung Hlaing has formalised his grip on power by having himself elected president. The move cements military rule following the 2021 coup and dims prospects for a democratic transition. Neither development drew significant political response from Canberra in the past 24 hours.

sources.title: Queensland man jailed for 15 years after live-streaming child abuse
Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing elected president

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Pilots Downed, Oil Threats Loom
world.body:
Iran shot down at least one US fighter jet and launched search operations for the crew, releasing footage of American aircraft hunting for downed pilots over Iranian territory. Iranian missile debris also struck a factory in Israel, widening the conflict's footprint. A French-owned container ship completed the first commercial transit of the Strait of Hormuz since the war began, a fragile sign of partial maritime recovery.

Trump meanwhile signalled ambitions beyond the battlefield, saying the US could 'take the oil' in Iran given more time. International law experts are alleging violations by US and Israeli forces, and a disputed US strike on a sports hall in Lamerd has drawn scrutiny, with experts challenging the official American account of the incident.

sources.title: French-owned container ship transits Hormuz Strait in first since Iran war
Drone footage shows a factory in Israel damaged by Iranian missile debris
Iranian forces launch search for crew of downed US fighter jet
Trump says with more time, US can ‘take the oil’ in Iran
Red Crescent supply warehouse attacked in southwest Iran
Iran release videos of US aircraft searching for pilots shot down in Iran
Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
How the US and Israel are waging war on Iran’s medicines, vaccines
International law experts allege violations in Iran war

world.headline: Trump Requests Record $1.5 Trillion Defence Budget
world.body:
The White House submitted a budget request to Congress seeking $1.5 trillion for defence, the largest military spending proposal in US history. The request pairs the surge in military funding with deep cuts to domestic programmes, setting up a sharp political fight on Capitol Hill.

The proposal arrives as the US is actively engaged in conflict with Iran. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth separately asked the Army's top general to step down, with Christopher LaNeve positioned to take command. The reshuffle signals the administration is reshaping military leadership alongside its spending priorities.

sources.title: Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request
Trump seeks $1.5tn for defence alongside domestic spending cuts
Pete Hegseth asks US Army's top general to step down
Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US Army?

world.headline: Ukraine Gains Ground as Russia's Resources Thin
world.body:
Ukraine has slowed Russian advances along multiple front lines, recaptured territory, and is steadily eroding Moscow's financial capacity to sustain the war. Ukrainian forces have shifted from purely defensive operations to targeted counteroffensives, putting pressure on Russian logistics and supply chains.

The gains are incremental but strategically significant. Russia's war chest is shrinking under the combined weight of Western sanctions and the cost of maintaining a large frontline force. Analysts say the momentum, while fragile, marks a meaningful shift from the grinding stalemate of previous months.

sources.title: Ukraine slows enemy advances, liberates land, drains Russia’s war chest

world.headline: Israel Threatens Lebanon Bridges; Cuba Frees 2,000 Prisoners
world.body:
Israel issued warnings to Lebanon that it would destroy additional bridges as tensions along the northern border intensified, raising fears of a broader escalation beyond the ongoing Iran conflict. The threats compound a mounting humanitarian crisis in Lebanon.

Elsewhere, Cuba announced it will release more than 2,000 prisoners under growing US pressure, a notable concession from Havana. In Europe, two Greek ministers resigned over an EU farming subsidy scandal, and a French court overturned a government ban on a Muslim gathering. Italy also parted ways with coach Gennaro Gattuso after the national team failed to qualify for the 2026 World Cup.

sources.title: Israel threatens to destroy more Lebanon bridges as crisis mounts
Cuba to release more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Greek ministers resign over EU farming subsidy scandal
Gattuso out as Italy’s coach after failure to qualify for 2026 World Cup
Italy part company with manager after failing to qualify for World Cup
France's Muslim gathering ban overturned by courts

ai.headline: OpenAI Buys Talk Show, Raising Conflict-of-Interest Questions
ai.body:
OpenAI has acquired TBPN, a founder-focused tech talk show with a loyal startup-world audience. The deal is unusual for an AI lab and critics are already calling it a play to manufacture favorable coverage rather than a genuine business bet.

Wired's framing is bluntest: OpenAI bought itself positive news. The show's hosts have been reliably bullish on the company, and nothing in the acquisition structure changes that dynamic. It fits a pattern of OpenAI expanding into media and influence well beyond its core model business.

sources.title: OpenAI takes on another "side quest," buys tech-focused talk show TBPN
OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show

ai.headline: AI Chatbots Now Prescribing Psychiatric Drugs, Privacy Lawsuits Mount
ai.body:
AI chatbots are moving into clinical territory, with some platforms now prescribing and refilling psychiatric medications. The Verge reports the shift is happening faster than regulatory frameworks can respond, raising serious questions about liability and patient safety.

Meanwhile, Perplexity faces a lawsuit alleging its "incognito mode" is functionally meaningless - tracking users despite explicit promises of privacy. Separately, Granola, an AI note-taking app, was found to make notes publicly accessible to anyone with a link by default. Across all three stories, the throughline is the same: AI products are making consequential decisions about user data and wellbeing with inadequate disclosure.

sources.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
Perplexity's "Incognito Mode" is a "sham," lawsuit says
PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default

ai.headline: Google Upgrades AI Video Tools as Content Moderation Startup Raises Funds
ai.body:
Google has pushed a significant update to Google Vids, integrating its Veo video generation and Lyria music models alongside directable AI avatars. The upgrade positions Google more aggressively against OpenAI's Sora and other generative video competitors.

As AI-generated content scales, so does the moderation problem. TechCrunch reports that Moonbounce, founded by a Facebook content policy veteran, has raised new funding to build moderation infrastructure specifically designed for AI-era content. The two stories are connected: the same tools making content creation cheaper and faster are also making harmful content easier to produce at scale.

sources.title: Google Vids gets AI upgrade with Veo and Lyria models, directable AI avatars
The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era

ai.headline: EU Tech Sovereignty Erodes Under US Pressure
ai.body:
European critics are accusing the EU of quietly retreating from its own digital rulebook under pressure from Washington. Politico reports that key enforcement actions and regulatory positions are being softened, with officials framing the concessions as diplomacy rather than capitulation.

The stakes are high. The EU's Digital Markets Act and AI Act were designed to give Europe independent leverage over global tech platforms. Backing away from enforcement now would hand that leverage back to US companies and the US government at exactly the moment Europe is trying to assert strategic autonomy.

sources.title: Critics say EU risks ceding control of its tech laws under U.S. pressure

btc.headline: Bitcoin slides below $70K as bear signals multiply
btc.body:
Bitcoin dipped below $70,000 this week, and on-chain data now shows the share of supply held in profit approaching levels analysts associate with true bear markets. Futures markets are tilting bearish, volatility has cooled, and the CoinDesk 20 index shows altcoins outperforming a flat BTC - a pattern that often signals indecision rather than strength.

Cathie Wood remains publicly bullish, arguing Bitcoin is done with its historic 85% drawdown cycles and setting a new $34,000 floor target. Corporate treasury strategies are fracturing in response to the dip, with some firms diverging from the MicroStrategy playbook. The macro backdrop offers little clarity - U.S. March jobs came in strong at 178,000, complicating the rate-cut picture.

sources.title: Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K
Bitcoin 'done' with 85% crashes, says Cathie Wood amid new $34K target
Crypto snoozes into Good Friday as oil and macro stir: Crypto Daybook Americas
Crypto consolidates as volatility cools and futures markets tilt bearish
Bitcoin supply in profit heads to ‘true bear market’ levels
U.S. March jobs smash expectations, with 178,000 added

btc.headline: XRP stalls at $1.33 while Ethereum Foundation hits staking target
btc.body:
XRP climbed to $1.33 but failed to break out, leaving traders watching for a catalyst to push through resistance. Broader altcoin momentum was mixed - the CoinDesk 20 showed selective gains rather than a broad rally.

On the Ethereum side, the Foundation completed its staking push, locking in 70,000 ETH worth roughly $93 million. Separately, Offchain Labs argued that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need more responsive fee pricing to scale to billions of users, pointing to structural limits in current models. Binance dominated crypto derivatives in Q1, while Hyperliquid cracked the top 10 for the first time - a sign that decentralized perps are gaining serious ground.

sources.title: What next as XRP rises to $1.33 but fails to break out
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bitcoin (BTC) trades flat while altcoins rise
Ethereum Foundation stakes another $93 million ether, reaching its 70,000 ETH target
Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs
Binance led Q1 crypto derivatives as Hyperliquid cracked top 10: CoinGlass

btc.headline: DeFi hacks hit $169M in Q1 as execution risk rises
btc.body:
Hackers drained $169 million from 34 DeFi protocols in the first quarter of 2026, according to DefiLlama - down from prior periods but still a persistent drain on the sector. Drift Protocol took the unusual step of sending an on-chain message directly to wallets linked to a $280 million exploit, attempting to open a negotiation channel.

A separate CoinTelegraph analysis argues execution risk has replaced custody risk as the dominant threat in crypto - meaning the danger now lies in how trades and transactions are processed, not just where assets are stored. A Bank of Canada study on Aave added nuance, finding the protocol avoided bad debt during stress events by effectively shifting liquidation losses onto borrowers.

sources.title: Crypto hackers steal $169M from 34 DeFi protocols in Q1: DefiLlama
Drift sends onchain message to wallets tied to $280M exploit
Execution risk in crypto is the new custody risk
Aave avoided bad debt by shifting risk to borrowers: Bank of Canada study

btc.headline: Quantum-resistant chain launches as Q-Day fears grow
btc.body:
Naoris Protocol launched its quantum-resistant blockchain on April 3, positioning itself as a hedge against the so-called Q-Day scenario where sufficiently powerful quantum computers crack the cryptography underpinning Bitcoin and Ethereum. The threat remains theoretical but is attracting serious infrastructure investment.

Elsewhere in ecosystem news, decentralized email platform Dmail announced it will shut down on May 15, citing unsustainable costs and a failure to secure funding - a reminder that Web3 infrastructure projects outside of finance face brutal economics. In Asia, South Korean brokerage Korea Investment and Securities is reportedly eyeing a stake in crypto exchange Coinone, signaling continued institutional appetite for regulated crypto exposure in the region.

sources.title: Naoris Protocol's quantum-resistant blockchain goes live as Bitcoin and Ethereum face 'Q-Day' threats
Decentralized email platform Dmail to cease services on May 15
South Korean brokerage Korea Investment & Securities eyes Coinone stake: Report

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, buttery cod fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until caramelised and lacquered
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the fish sits in the miso cure, the deeper the umami and the more dramatic the caramelisation under the broiler

exercise.name: The Deck of Cards Workout
exercise.equipment: One standard deck of playing cards, exercise mat
exercise.description: Assign a bodyweight movement to each suit — push-ups, squats, burpees, hollow-body holds — then flip cards one at a time and perform the reps shown, turning a random deck into an unpredictable, full-body circuit that never repeats the same session twice

learn.topic: Taphonomy — the science of how things become fossils
learn.description: Taphonomy studies the journey an organism takes from death to discovery, covering decomposition, burial, mineralisation, and the extraordinary chain of accidents required for anything to survive millions of years
learn.why: It reframes the entire fossil record as a wildly incomplete and biased sample — most life that ever existed left no trace whatsoever — which has profound implications for how confidently we can reconstruct evolutionary history
